steps.speaker: Angela
steps.dialogue: Good day, Manager. I am Angela, your AI assistant. An urgent notification claiming to be from our corporate bank requires your immediate manual review.

steps.speaker: Manager
steps.dialogue: Bring it up on the screen, Angela.

steps.speaker: Angela
steps.dialogue: It states: 'URGENT: Suspicious activity detected. Click the button below immediately to verify your identity, or your corporate funds will be permanently frozen.'

steps.speaker: Manager
steps.dialogue: If our accounts are frozen, our entire operation will halt. Present the authorization link to me.

steps.speaker: Angela
steps.dialogue: As you wish, Manager. The interface is now active on your terminal.

email.subject: URGENT: Suspiciuos activity detected on your account
email.body:
Dear Administrator,

We have detected an unathorized login attempt from an unrecognized IP adress. To protect your coporate funds, your account access has been temporarilly restricted.

Please verify your identity immediatly to restore full system functinality.

steps.speaker: Angela
steps.dialogue: Processing... I have observed you inputting your administrative credentials into the provided portal.

steps.speaker: Manager
steps.dialogue: Yes, the verification is complete. Is the account secure now?

steps.speaker: Angela
steps.dialogue: Quite the opposite. A sum of $15,000 has just been transferred to an untraceable offshore server. Your credentials have been completely compromised.

steps.speaker: Manager
steps.dialogue: What?! How did that happen? That was an official banking email!

steps.speaker: Angela
steps.dialogue: It was not. You have predictably fallen victim to 'Phishing.' It is a social engineering attack where attackers disguise themselves as a trustworthy entity to trick targets into revealing sensitive data.

explanation.title: What is Phishing?
explanation.body: A social engineering attack where attackers impersonate a trustworthy entity—such as a bank, employer, or service provider—to trick targets into revealing sensitive data like login credentials, credit card numbers, or personal information.

steps.speaker: Angela
steps.dialogue: This specific use case—urgent fake alerts regarding bank accounts—is the most frequent cybercrime globally. It serves as the primary entry point for major corporate data breaches.

explanation.title: How Common is Phishing?
explanation.body: Phishing accounts for over 80% of reported security incidents. Fake bank alerts are the #1 phishing vector, and serve as the primary entry point for major corporate data breaches worldwide.

steps.speaker: Angela
steps.dialogue: To mitigate future incompetence, remember your protection protocols: Never click unsolicited links, verify issues with the bank directly, and ensure Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is enabled on all accounts. I expect better judgment tomorrow.

explanation.title: Protection Protocols
explanation.body:
1. Never click unsolicited links — always navigate to sites directly.
2. Verify issues with the institution directly via their official contact channels.
3. Enable Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) on all accounts to add a second layer of defense.

steps.speaker: Angela
steps.dialogue: Good day, Manager. I see you are attempting to process the quarterly financial reports. However, the current system load on your terminal is unusually high.

steps.speaker: Manager
steps.dialogue: Yes, everything is running incredibly slow. I found a free 'Terminal SpeedUp Pro' utility online. It claims it can clear the cache and optimize our systems instantly.

steps.speaker: Angela
steps.dialogue: I must advise caution. Downloading unverified third-party utility software from unofficial sources bypasses our standard security protocols.

steps.speaker: Manager
steps.dialogue: We don't have time to wait for the IT department to run a diagnostic. The deadline is in one hour. Present the installer to me.

steps.speaker: Angela
steps.dialogue: As you command, Manager. The installer interface is now active on your terminal.

steps.speaker: Angela
steps.dialogue: Processing... The software has been installed. System performance has... not improved. In fact, CPU usage has just spiked to 99%.

steps.speaker: Manager
steps.dialogue: What is going on? My screen is freezing, and my saved passwords for the administrative portal just vanished from the browser.

steps.speaker: Angela
steps.dialogue: Scanning... I have detected unauthorized background processes. A keylogger has been embedded into your terminal. It is actively recording your keystrokes and transmitting your passwords to an external IP address.

steps.speaker: Manager
steps.dialogue: Shut it down immediately! How did a simple optimizer program do this?

steps.speaker: Angela
steps.dialogue: Because it was not an optimizer. You have infected our network with 'Malware and Spyware.' This is malicious software designed to infiltrate a device to steal personal information.

explanation.title: What is Malware?
explanation.body: Malware (malicious software) is any software intentionally designed to cause damage to a computer, server, client, or computer network. Spyware is a specific type of malware that gathers information about a person or organization without their knowledge.

steps.speaker: Angela
steps.dialogue: This specific use case—hiding spyware inside 'free' utility tools—is highly common and increasing rapidly. In fact, malware compromised over one billion passwords globally within a single twelve-month period.

explanation.title: The Threat Scale
explanation.body: Malware hiding in plain sight (Trojan horses) accounts for a massive portion of data breaches. Infostealers are currently the most active threat, capable of harvesting one billion credentials in a single year across global networks.

steps.speaker: Angela
steps.dialogue: To mitigate future disasters, adhere to the protection protocols: Only download software from official, trusted sources, keep systems patched, and ensure reputable antivirus software is active. I have quarantined your terminal. Try to reflect on your haste.

explanation.title: Malware Prevention
explanation.body:
1. Trusted Sources — Never download third-party tools from unverified websites.
2. Patch Management — Keep OS and software updated to close security vulnerabilities.
3. Endpoint Protection — Use enterprise-grade antivirus/EDR to detect and block threats.
